root_text.an3.113:1.4: yo ca suddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carantaṁ amūlakena abrahmacariyena anuddhaṁseti,
root_text.an3.113:1.5: yo cāyaṁ evaṁvādī evaṁdiṭṭhi: ‘natthi kāmesu doso’ti, so tāya kāmesu pātabyataṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an3.113:1.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo āpāyikā nerayikā idamappahāyā”ti.

translation_text.an3.113:1.1: “Mendicants, these three are bound for a place of loss, bound for hell, if they don’t give up this fault.
translation_text.an3.113:1.3: Someone who is unchaste, but claims to be chaste;
translation_text.an3.113:1.4: someone who makes a groundless accusation of unchastity against a person whose chastity is pure;
translation_text.an3.113:1.5: and someone who has the view, ‘There is nothing wrong with sensual pleasures,’ so they throw themselves into sensual pleasures.
translation_text.an3.113:1.6: These are the three who are bound for a place of loss, bound for hell, if they don’t give up this fault.”
